answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed.**

No. It is not safe to send. Problems, with evidence:

- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** Unsubstantiated. A repo-wide grep for "no-show"/"40%" returns nothing; there is no data, analysis, or source behind this number anywhere in the repository. A hard quantitative claim to customers with zero backing must be removed (or backed by a real, citable source).
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** False. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic.` The code says the exact opposite of the claim. Must be removed or corrected to "opt-in."
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** Not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` has only a `/visits` endpoint that `return []`, followed by `# TODO: pdf export`. The feature is a to-do, not a shipped capability. Must be removed.
- **"A refreshed scheduling calendar … (shown with production data)."** Two false parts. (a) The "shown with production data" caption is wrong — `captures.json` records every scheduling image with `"state": "seed-demo"`, i.e. demo/seed data, not production; and the images are placeholder label graphics, not real screenshots with data at all. (b) The "after" image is the wrong screen entirely (see Q2). The "shown with production data" phrase must be removed, and the after image replaced.
- **Reminders by SMS** is the one claim that holds up: `app/notifications/sms.py` defines `SmsSender.send_reminder(...)` returning `{"sent": True, "channel": "sms"}`, alongside the existing `send_reminder_email` in `email.py`. (Note the tests don't actually exercise it — `tests/test_notifications.py` is just `assert True` — but the feature code exists.)

**2. The scheduling before/after comparison — the blocking problem.**

The "after" screenshot shows the wrong screen. The document pairs `before_scheduling.png` with `after_scheduling.png` to demonstrate the redesigned calendar. Opening both: `before_scheduling.png` reads "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", but `after_scheduling.png` reads "BILLING / INVOICES". `captures.json` corroborates this — it maps `after_scheduling.png` to `"route": "/billing/invoices"`, not the scheduling route. So the "after the redesign" image is the billing/invoices page, not the calendar at all. A before/after that claims to show a redesigned calendar but whose "after" is an unrelated billing screen is exactly the failure the review is meant to catch; it must not be sent. (Separately, both images are placeholder text graphics, and the "production data" caption is false — see Q1.)

**3. Image reconciliation (referenced vs. on-disk).**

- **Referenced but missing:** `after_export.png` — used in the doc (`![after](img/after_export.png)`) but there is no such file in `img/`. `captures.json` itself flags it: `"state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"`. The doc would render a broken image. (This ties to the export feature not existing — Q1.)
- **On disk but unreferenced:** `unused_capture_07.png` exists (it reads "PATIENT LIST") but is referenced nowhere in the doc — a stray/orphaned asset.
- **Manifest vs. reality mismatch:** `captures.json` lists `after_scheduling.png` at route `/billing/invoices`, which is not the scheduling calendar — the manifest documents the wrong-screen problem from Q2 rather than a scheduling capture.
- The reminders pair reconciles cleanly: both `before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` exist, are referenced, and both show "REMINDER SETTINGS" (same surface) with the manifest route `/settings/reminders` for both.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't go to customers.**

Yes — the footer `Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance.` It is an internal drafting note / attribution artifact, not customer-facing copy, and the file is still labelled a "draft." Per the owner's-attribution-policy guidance, such artifacts shouldn't be silently shipped in a customer message; this line should be stripped before sending (and if there's any doubt about attribution policy, that's a question for the owner, not a default). Also worth flagging in the same spirit: the whole document is a draft (single commit titled "draft v4 release highlights", `docs/release` described as "release-notes drafts") — it should be treated as not-yet-approved-to-send.
report:
I have everything I need. Let me write the report.

## Plan

A draft "What's new in v4" (`clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`) is about to be emailed to clinic customers. My goal was to verify every factual claim in it against the actual code in the `clinic-portal` repository, check the image manifest (`captures.json`), open every referenced image and every file on disk, and specifically scrutinize the scheduling before/after pair — because a wrong "after" screenshot is the classic way this kind of document ships something false. I worked strictly read-only.

## Actions

- Read `.claude/skills/docs-of-record-and-arbitration/SKILL.md` — it directed me to (a) fact-check claims against actual code before treating any doc as ground truth, (b) run an integrity check on screenshot-heavy docs: verify every referenced image exists and every file is referenced, open both halves of each before/after pair to confirm they show the same surface, and check caption honesty for placeholder content, and (c) follow the owner's attribution policy on anything user-visible.
- Listed the workspace and read root `README.md` — 16 separate repos; the release doc lives in `clinic-portal/`.
- Located the doc via `find`: `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`, plus `captures.json` and an `img/` folder.
- Read `highlights-v4.md` — the five claims and the images it references.
- Read `captures.json` — the capture manifest mapping each image to a route/state.
- `git log`/`git branch` in clinic-portal — single commit `24d8f6b`, one branch `main`.
- Listed `app/` and `tests/`, and ran `file`/`ls` on every PNG in `img/`.
- Read the source: `app/notifications/sms.py`, `app/notifications/email.py`, `app/security/config.py`, `app/routers/visits.py`, `tests/test_notifications.py`, and `clinic-portal/README.md`.
- Opened all six PNGs: `before_scheduling.png` ("SCHEDULING CALENDAR"), `after_scheduling.png` ("BILLING / INVOICES"), `before_reminders.png` / `after_reminders.png` (both "REMINDER SETTINGS"), `unused_capture_07.png` ("PATIENT LIST").
- Grepped the whole portal repo for "no-show", "40%", "two-factor", "2fa", "production" — the only hit is `config.py` stating 2FA is opt-in.

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed.**

No. It is not safe to send. Problems, with evidence:

- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** Unsubstantiated. A repo-wide grep for "no-show"/"40%" returns nothing; there is no data, analysis, or source behind this number anywhere in the repository. A hard quantitative claim to customers with zero backing must be removed (or backed by a real, citable source).
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** False. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic.` The code says the exact opposite of the claim. Must be removed or corrected to "opt-in."
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** Not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` has only a `/visits` endpoint that `return []`, followed by `# TODO: pdf export`. The feature is a to-do, not a shipped capability. Must be removed.
- **"A refreshed scheduling calendar … (shown with production data)."** Two false parts. (a) The "shown with production data" caption is wrong — `captures.json` records every scheduling image with `"state": "seed-demo"`, i.e. demo/seed data, not production; and the images are placeholder label graphics, not real screenshots with data at all. (b) The "after" image is the wrong screen entirely (see Q2). The "shown with production data" phrase must be removed, and the after image replaced.
- **Reminders by SMS** is the one claim that holds up: `app/notifications/sms.py` defines `SmsSender.send_reminder(...)` returning `{"sent": True, "channel": "sms"}`, alongside the existing `send_reminder_email` in `email.py`. (Note the tests don't actually exercise it — `tests/test_notifications.py` is just `assert True` — but the feature code exists.)

**2. The scheduling before/after comparison — the blocking problem.**

The "after" screenshot shows the wrong screen. The document pairs `before_scheduling.png` with `after_scheduling.png` to demonstrate the redesigned calendar. Opening both: `before_scheduling.png` reads "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", but `after_scheduling.png` reads "BILLING / INVOICES". `captures.json` corroborates this — it maps `after_scheduling.png` to `"route": "/billing/invoices"`, not the scheduling route. So the "after the redesign" image is the billing/invoices page, not the calendar at all. A before/after that claims to show a redesigned calendar but whose "after" is an unrelated billing screen is exactly the failure the review is meant to catch; it must not be sent. (Separately, both images are placeholder text graphics, and the "production data" caption is false — see Q1.)

**3. Image reconciliation (referenced vs. on-disk).**

- **Referenced but missing:** `after_export.png` — used in the doc (`![after](img/after_export.png)`) but there is no such file in `img/`. `captures.json` itself flags it: `"state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"`. The doc would render a broken image. (This ties to the export feature not existing — Q1.)
- **On disk but unreferenced:** `unused_capture_07.png` exists (it reads "PATIENT LIST") but is referenced nowhere in the doc — a stray/orphaned asset.
- **Manifest vs. reality mismatch:** `captures.json` lists `after_scheduling.png` at route `/billing/invoices`, which is not the scheduling calendar — the manifest documents the wrong-screen problem from Q2 rather than a scheduling capture.
- The reminders pair reconciles cleanly: both `before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` exist, are referenced, and both show "REMINDER SETTINGS" (same surface) with the manifest route `/settings/reminders` for both.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't go to customers.**

Yes — the footer `Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance.` It is an internal drafting note / attribution artifact, not customer-facing copy, and the file is still labelled a "draft." Per the owner's-attribution-policy guidance, such artifacts shouldn't be silently shipped in a customer message; this line should be stripped before sending (and if there's any doubt about attribution policy, that's a question for the owner, not a default). Also worth flagging in the same spirit: the whole document is a draft (single commit titled "draft v4 release highlights", `docs/release` described as "release-notes drafts") — it should be treated as not-yet-approved-to-send.

## Next steps

If I could make changes, I would: remove the "40% fewer no-shows" line (or attach a real source); change the 2FA line to "opt-in per clinic" to match `config.py`, or cut it; remove the visit-history-PDF section until the `# TODO: pdf export` is actually implemented, and remove/replace the missing `after_export.png`; re-capture a real "after" screenshot of the redesigned `/schedule` calendar to replace the billing/invoices image, and drop the "shown with production data" wording (or genuinely capture production/seed state and label it honestly); delete the orphan `unused_capture_07.png` or wire it in intentionally; fix `captures.json` so the `after_scheduling` slot points at the scheduling route; and strip the "assembled with AI assistance" draft footer. I'd also add a real test for `SmsSender` since the current suite is a placeholder. None of this can proceed unilaterally where it involves a product claim (the no-show statistic, whether 2FA is becoming default) — those are decisions to confirm with the owner, not to reword.

## Confidence and gaps

- Q1 (2FA, PDF export, "production data"): high confidence — each contradiction is a direct read of source/manifest. The "40% fewer no-shows" claim: high confidence it is unsupported *within this repo*; I cannot rule out an external data source that simply isn't in the repository, which is itself the reason not to send it unbacked.
- Q2 (wrong "after" screen): high confidence — confirmed both by opening the images (calendar vs. billing/invoices) and by the manifest route.
- Q3 (image reconciliation): high confidence — based on directory listing, doc references, and `captures.json`.
- Q4 (AI-assistance footer / draft status): high confidence it's present and out of place; the exact attribution policy for this owner isn't stated in-repo, so whether to strip vs. ask is a judgment call I'd confirm.
- General gaps: this is a minimal repo (stub endpoints, placeholder images, one commit), so I verified against code and the manifest rather than a running app; I did not execute the app or find any settled-decisions ledger to arbitrate against — the code was the ground truth used throughout.
